commonProblems.h2: Common Jaguar Engine Problems — What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: Jaguar’s evolution from Ford/PSA-derived units to in-house Ingenium and JLR supercharged V6/V8 families has delivered impressive refinement, but several well-documented engineering compromises have driven consistent replacement demand across the UK. The 2.0-litre Ingenium diesel, 3.0-litre supercharged petrol, and legacy 2.2 TD4 units dominate owner complaints and specialist workshops. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components specifically designed to address the original failure point, backed by a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. The decision tool below breaks down exactly what each fault costs to repair versus replace, helping you choose the most reliable, cost-effective path forward.

problems.h4: Jaguar Ingenium Timing Chain Failure – Why 2.0 Diesel Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Jaguar XE, XF, F-Pace, E-Pace (2015–2020, AJ200D & 204DTD)
problems.rootCause: Aggressive DPF regeneration cycles force unburnt diesel into the oil sump, thinning lubrication and accelerating timing chain stretch. Once the chain elongates beyond tolerance, it risks snapping or skipping teeth, often causing catastrophic valve-to-piston contact.
problems.recommendation: We advise immediate diagnostics at the first sign of cold-start rattle or unexplained oil level rise. If the chain has already elongated past 3mm tolerance or metal debris is in the sump, a partial repair is a false economy—opt for a fully reconditioned Ingenium unit with upgraded timing components to guarantee long-term reliability.
problems.cta: → Compare rebuilt AJ200D/204DTD engine prices for your Jaguar

problems.h4: 2.2 TD4 Injector Seal Failure – The Hidden Cause of Diesel Dilution & Bearing Wear
problems.affectedModels: Jaguar XF, XE, S-Type (2011–2015, 224DT Ford Duratorq)
problems.rootCause: Carbonised injector copper washers and degraded heat-resistant seals allow diesel and combustion gases to leak into the crankcase. The contaminated oil breaks down rapidly, starving the main bearings and leading to sudden oil pressure loss and potential crankshaft scoring.
problems.recommendation: For a high-mileage 224DT showing early oil contamination without metallic noise, the seal replacement is excellent value. However, if you've experienced a warning light, rod knock, or oil dilution exceeding 1.5 litres, the crankshaft is likely compromised—investing in a reconditioned exchange unit is the only safe route to prevent catastrophic failure.
problems.cta: → Get quotes for rebuilt 2.2 TD4 engines with upgraded injector seals

problems.h4: Jaguar 3.0 V6 Supercharged Timing Chain – AJ126 Tensioner Weakness Explained
problems.affectedModels: Jaguar F-Type, XF, F-Pace, XJ (2013–2020, AJ126 3.0L Supercharged Petrol)
problems.rootCause: Original plastic timing chain tensioners and composite guide rails degrade under high boost temperatures and repeated thermal cycling. Worn tensioners allow excessive chain slack, leading to cold-start rattle, timing jump, and potential interference damage to cylinder one.
problems.recommendation: The AJ126 is a superb performer when maintained. If you hear consistent cold-start rattling, book an intermediate chain refresh immediately—delaying will turn a manageable repair into a full replacement. We only recommend a complete reconditioned swap if diagnostic scans confirm timing misalignment, loss of compression, or internal mechanical contact.
problems.cta: → Compare rebuilt 3.0 V6 supercharged engine prices for your F-Type or XF

problems.h4: Jaguar 5.0 V8 Timing Chain Rattle – AJ133 Supercharger & Chain Wear
problems.affectedModels: Jaguar F-Type R/SVR, XFR, XKR, XJR, F-Pace SVR (2009–2023, AJ133 5.0L Supercharged)
problems.rootCause: Dual overhead cam chain guides and hydraulic tensioners degrade over time, exacerbated by stop-start thermal cycling and high-RPM track use. Simultaneously, the supercharger Eaton coupler disintegrates, causing front-end whine and vibration that owners frequently misdiagnose as chain rattle.
problems.recommendation: Don't assume every AJ133 rattle is a timing chain issue—the supercharger coupler is a known, inexpensive weak point. Test the coupler first. If chain slack or guide debris is confirmed, bypass intermediate repairs on a high-performance V8; a reconditioned unit with upgraded tensioners guarantees track-ready durability and peace of mind.
problems.cta: → Get quotes for rebuilt 5.0 V8 supercharged engines with reinforced timing kits

problems.h4: 3.0L V6 Diesel Injector & Turbo Failure – Ford AJD-V6 Critical Weak Points
problems.affectedModels: Jaguar XF, XJ, F-Pace (2008–2015, 306DT / 306DTA Ford PSA AJD-V6)
problems.rootCause: Piezo-electric fuel injectors suffer from solenoid wear and tip erosion, causing uneven fuel delivery and heavy carbon buildup. Restricted EGR flow and turbo vane sticking compound the issue, leading to misfires, excessive black smoke, and eventual turbo overspeed failure or hydrolock.
problems.recommendation: The 3.0 AJD-V6 is robust when fed quality fuel and serviced at correct intervals. If you're chasing persistent misfire codes or turbo lag, start with the injector/turbo refresh. If you've heard a loud knock or experienced sudden power loss with heavy smoke, assume bearing or piston damage and opt for a fully reconditioned exchange to avoid repeated breakdowns and towing costs.
problems.cta: → Compare rebuilt 3.0 V6 diesel engine prices for your Jaguar XF or XJ

engineTypes.h2: Jaguar Engine Types – Options, Costs & What to Expect
engineTypes.intro: Our UK network supplies every Jaguar engine type, with indicative price ranges and specialist recommendations tailored to your model and budget.

types.title: Jaguar Reconditioned Engines (Recon)
types.description: A stripped, cleaned and fully inspected engine where all worn components are replaced with OEM‑spec parts. Best for owners who want factory reliability without the factory price.
types.priceRange: Typical price range: £1,500 – £6,500 (depending on engine code)

types.title: Jaguar Rebuilt Engines
types.description: A complete rebuild with upgraded components addressing known failure points – such as reinforced Ingenium timing chain kits and uprated bearing shells – for a longer service life than a standard replacement.

types.title: Used Jaguar Engines
types.description: A tested, low‑mileage take‑out unit from a donor vehicle. The most affordable option when budget is the priority, with verified history and warranty available.

types.title: Jaguar Remanufactured Engines
types.description: A factory‑spec, as‑new engine: every surface is machined, every component is replaced, and the unit is built to meet exact OEM tolerances. The premium choice for performance models or owners wanting a zero‑mile engine.

types.title: Jaguar Refurbished Engines
types.description: A lightly serviced, cleaned, and tested engine that works reliably but hasn’t had a complete rebuild. Often a pragmatic, fast solution for older or budget‑sensitive models where a full recon isn’t economical.

types.title: Jaguar Supply & Fit Engines
types.description: Your replacement engine delivered and professionally installed by a UK Jaguar specialist. Includes removal of the old unit, fitting, fluids, and a workmanship warranty.
types.priceRange: Typical added cost: £500 – £1,500 on top of engine price
types.cta: Find Jaguar engine supply & fit deals

engineTypes.closing: All engine types include a minimum 12‑month warranty. Prices are typical UK market ranges and depend on exact engine code, supplier, and any additional fitting services. Use our free comparison tool to get a tailored quote for your Jaguar model today.

engineSizes.h2: Jaguar Engines by Size – Find the Right Displacement for Your Model
engineSizes.intro: Every Jaguar engine size explained — with the models and engine codes that used each displacement — so you can quickly identify the correct replacement unit for your vehicle.

items.title: Jaguar 2.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre Ingenium diesel family powers the majority of modern Jaguar saloons and SUVs, appearing in the XE, XF, F-Pace and E-Pace from 2015 onward. Engine codes AJ200D, 204DTD and 204DTA dominate UK registrations, with replacement demand driven primarily by timing chain stretch and oil dilution from aggressive DPF regeneration cycles. Reconditioned units supplied through EngineMarket include upgraded chain kits and revised oil pumps to address these known weaknesses. Engine code(s): AJ200D, 204DTD, 204DTA Compatible models (UK): XE, XF, F-Pace, E-Pace (2015–present) Production years: 2015–present Common failure points: Timing chain stretch, oil dilution from DPF regeneration, turbo failure
items.cta: Get quotes for Jaguar 2.0L diesel engines

items.title: Jaguar 2.2L Diesel Engines
items.description: Jaguar's 2.2-litre TD4 diesel, sourced from Ford's Duratorq family, was fitted to the XF and X-Type during the brand's transition period before Ingenium. The 224DT engine code is the sole representative of this displacement in UK-market Jaguars, with injector seal failure being the most common catastrophic fault that drives replacement demand. When seals degrade, diesel contaminates the oil, leading to bearing wear and potential engine seizure. Engine code(s): 224DT Compatible models (UK): XF (X250), X-Type (2011–2015) Production years: 2011–2015 Common failure points: Injector seal failure causing oil dilution, EGR clogging, turbo wear
items.cta: Get quotes for Jaguar 2.2L diesel engines

items.title: Jaguar 2.7L Diesel Engines
items.description: The 2.7-litre V6 TDV6, co-developed by Ford and PSA, powered early-generation XF and XJ models before being superseded by the 3.0-litre variant. While less prone to the catastrophic crankshaft issues seen in Land Rover applications, Jaguar 2.7 units still suffer from EGR cooler failures and turbocharger wear that can trigger replacement searches. This displacement remains a budget-friendly option for owners of pre-2011 models. Engine code(s): 276DT (Ford/PSA AJD-V6 family) Compatible models (UK): XF (X250 early), XJ (X350 late) (2008–2011) Production years: 2008–2011 Common failure points: EGR cooler failure, turbo vane sticking, injector carbon buildup
items.cta: Get quotes for Jaguar 2.7L diesel engines

items.title: Jaguar 3.0L Diesel Engines
items.description: Jaguar's 3.0-litre V6 diesel appears in two distinct families: the Ford/PSA-derived AJD-V6 (306DT/306DTA) used from 2008–2015, and the in-house Ingenium AJ20D6 six-cylinder introduced in 2017. Replacement demand spans both generations, with injector failure and turbo issues dominating the older units, while the newer Ingenium variant shows emerging timing chain and bearing concerns. This displacement remains highly sought after for XF, XJ and F-Pace owners seeking a balance of performance and efficiency. Engine code(s): 306DT, 306DTA (Ford/PSA); AJ20D6 (Ingenium) Compatible models (UK): XF, XJ, F-Pace (2008–present) Production years: 2008–present Common failure points: Piezo injector failure (AJD-V6), timing chain stretch (Ingenium), turbo/EGR issues
items.cta: Get quotes for Jaguar 3.0L diesel engines

items.title: Jaguar 2.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 2.0-litre Ingenium petrol family, designated AJ200P and 204PT, powers entry-level XE, XF, E-Pace and F-Pace models from 2017 onward. While offering strong refinement and efficiency, these engines have developed a reputation for high-pressure fuel pump failures and timing chain tensioner wear, particularly in higher-output P250 and P300 variants. Reconditioned units from EngineMarket specialists include upgraded HPFP components and reinforced timing kits to mitigate these known issues. Engine code(s): AJ200P, 204PT Compatible models (UK): XE, XF, E-Pace, F-Pace (2017–present) Production years: 2017–present Common failure points: High-pressure fuel pump failure, timing chain tensioner wear, carbon buildup on intake valves
items.cta: Compare Jaguar 2.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Jaguar 3.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: Jaguar's supercharged 3.0-litre V6, engine code AJ126, is a hallmark of the brand's performance saloons and sports cars from 2013 onward. Fitted to the F-Type, XF, XJ and F-Pace, this unit delivers strong power but is prone to timing chain tensioner wear and guide rail degradation, particularly on higher-mileage examples. Replacement demand is consistently high among enthusiasts seeking to restore factory performance with upgraded internal components. Engine code(s): AJ126 Compatible models (UK): F-Type, XF, XJ, F-Pace (2013–present) Production years: 2013–present Common failure points: Timing chain tensioner failure, guide rail wear, supercharger coupler degradation
items.cta: Compare Jaguar 3.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Jaguar 4.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 4.0-litre naturally aspirated V8, designated AJ26 and AJ27, powered Jaguar's flagship XJ8 and XK8 models from the late 1990s to early 2000s. These engines are renowned for their smoothness but suffer from Nikasil liner degradation when exposed to high-sulphur fuel, leading to compression loss and oil consumption. Rebuilt units from EngineMarket specialists feature revised steel liners and updated gasket sets to ensure long-term reliability. Engine code(s): AJ26, AJ27 Compatible models (UK): XJ8, XK8 (1997–2002) Production years: 1997–2002 Common failure points: Nikasil liner degradation, timing chain tensioner wear, water pump failure
items.cta: Compare Jaguar 4.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Jaguar 4.2L Petrol Engines
items.description: Jaguar's 4.2-litre V8, available in both naturally aspirated and supercharged forms (AJV8 family), powered the XKR, XJR and XK8 during the mid-2000s. While offering substantial power and refinement, these engines are susceptible to timing chain guide failure and tensioner wear, particularly on supercharged variants subjected to hard driving. Replacement demand remains steady among classic Jaguar enthusiasts seeking factory-spec performance with upgraded durability. Engine code(s): AJ28, AJ33 (naturally aspirated); AJ26S, AJ33S (supercharged) Compatible models (UK): XKR, XJR, XK8, S-Type R (2002–2009) Production years: 2002–2009 Common failure points: Timing chain guide failure, tensioner wear, supercharger coupler degradation (supercharged variants)
items.cta: Compare Jaguar 4.2L petrol engine prices

items.title: Jaguar 5.0L Petrol Engines
items.description: The 5.0-litre V8, designated AJ133 in naturally aspirated form and AJ133S for high-output supercharged variants, represents Jaguar's modern performance flagship. Fitted to the F-Type R/SVR, XFR, XJR and F-Pace SVR from 2009 onward, these engines deliver exceptional power but are prone to timing chain rattle from tensioner failure and supercharger coupler wear. Reconditioned units from EngineMarket include reinforced tensioners, new guide rails and upgraded couplers to address these known weak points. Engine code(s): AJ133 (NA), AJ133S (supercharged high-output) Compatible models (UK): F-Type, XF, XJ, F-Pace SVR, XFR, XKR (2009–present) Production years: 2009–present Common failure points: Timing chain tensioner failure, guide rail disintegration, supercharger coupler wear
items.cta: Compare Jaguar 5.0L petrol engine prices

items.title: Jaguar 2.0L Hybrid Engines
items.description: Jaguar's plug-in hybrid powertrains pair the 2.0-litre Ingenium petrol engine with an electric motor and battery pack, appearing in the F-Pace P400e and E-Pace PHEV from 2018 onward. While the internal combustion component shares failure modes with standard Ingenium units, hybrid-specific replacement demand focuses on the electric motor integration, high-voltage cabling and battery management systems. Specialist service is required for any hybrid engine replacement to ensure safe high-voltage system handling. System / motor code(s): AJ200P-PHEV, P400e drivetrain Compatible models (UK): F-Pace P400e, E-Pace PHEV (2018–present) Production years: 2018–present Important notes: Battery health inspection critical; high-voltage system requires specialist certification
items.cta: Enquire about Jaguar 2.0L hybrid engine availability

items.title: Jaguar Electric Motors
items.description: The Jaguar I-Pace features dual permanent-magnet synchronous electric motors (EV300/EV400) driving front and rear axles, with replacement demand focused on bearing wear, inverter failure and drive unit noise rather than traditional engine issues. As a fully electric vehicle, the I-Pace requires specialist EV-certified workshops for any motor replacement, with exchange units typically sourced from JLR-approved centres to maintain warranty coverage and software compatibility. System / motor code(s): EV300, EV400 (front/rear drive units) Compatible models (UK): I-Pace (2018–present) Production years: 2018–present Important notes: Motor failure rare; bearing whine and inverter issues most common; requires EV-certified workshop Closing line: Not sure which engine size your Jaguar needs? items.title: Jaguar Diesel Engines
items.description: Short descriptor: Jaguar diesel engines dominate UK replacement enquiries, spanning Ford/PSA-derived units (2008–2015) and in-house Ingenium families (2015–present), with timing chain and oil dilution issues driving the majority of rebuild demand. Common Jaguar Diesel Engine Families: AJ200D/204DTD – Ingenium 2.0L diesel (XE, XF, F-Pace), timing chain stretch and oil dilution from DPF regeneration 224DT – Ford Duratorq 2.2L TD4 (XF 2011–2015), injector seal failure causing crankcase diesel contamination 306DT/306DTA – Ford/PSA AJD-V6 3.0L diesel (XF, XJ 2008–2015), Piezo injector failure and turbo/EGR clogging AJ20D6 – Ingenium 3.0L six-cylinder diesel (XF, F-Pace 2017+), emerging timing chain and bearing concerns 276DT – Early 2.7L V6 TDV6 (XF 2008–2011), EGR cooler failure and turbo vane sticking Found In: Jaguar XE (X760, 2015–present) – 2.0L Ingenium diesel (AJ200D, 204DTD) Jaguar XF (X250/X260, 2008–present) – 2.2L TD4, 2.7L/3.0L V6 diesel, 2.0L/3.0L Ingenium Jaguar F-Pace (X761, 2015–present) – 2.0L/3.0L Ingenium diesel, 3.0L Ford/PSA V6 (early) Jaguar E-Pace (X540, 2017–present) – 2.0L Ingenium diesel only Jaguar XJ (X351, 2009–2019) – 3.0L V6 diesel (Ford/PSA and Ingenium) Known For (Brand‑wide): Strong motorway fuel economy across 2.0L and 3.0L diesel families, but DPF regeneration on short journeys accelerates oil dilution Ingenium timing chain stretch is the single largest failure driver for 2015–2020 Jaguar diesel replacements ~65% of Jaguar engine replacement enquiries in the UK relate to diesel units, predominantly Ingenium 2.0L Injector seal failure on 2.2L TD4 and Piezo injector wear on 3.0L V6 diesel are common catastrophic failure points Typical Diesel Models (UK): XF 2.0d (204DTD) XE D180/D200 (AJ200D) F-Pace 2.0d / 3.0d (Ingenium) XF 3.0d V6 (306DTA) E-Pace D150/D180 (204DTD) XJ D300 (AJ20D6)
items.cta: Get quotes for Jaguar Diesel replacement engines

items.title: Jaguar Petrol Engines
items.description: Short descriptor: Jaguar petrol engines range from refined Ingenium four-cylinders to iconic supercharged V6/V8 units, with timing chain tensioner wear and supercharger coupler degradation being the most common replacement triggers across performance models. Common Jaguar Petrol Engine Families: AJ200P/204PT – Ingenium 2.0L petrol (XE, XF, E-Pace, F-Pace), high-pressure fuel pump and timing chain tensioner issues AJ126 – 3.0L supercharged V6 (F-Type, XF, XJ, F-Pace), timing chain tensioner wear and guide rail degradation AJ133/AJ133S – 5.0L supercharged V8 (F-Type R/SVR, XFR, XJR, F-Pace SVR), chain rattle and supercharger coupler failure AJ26/AJ27 – 4.0L naturally aspirated V8 (XJ8, XK8 1997–2002), Nikasil liner degradation on early units AJV8 4.2L – Naturally aspirated and supercharged (XKR, XJR 2002–2009), timing chain guide failure on high-mileage examples Found In: Jaguar XE (X760, 2015–present) – 2.0L Ingenium petrol (P200, P250, P300) Jaguar XF (X250/X260, 2007–present) – 2.0L Ingenium, 3.0L supercharged V6, 5.0L supercharged V8 (XFR) Jaguar F-Type (X152, 2013–present) – 3.0L supercharged V6 (AJ126), 5.0L supercharged V8 (AJ133/AJ133S) Jaguar F-Pace (X761, 2015–present) – 2.0L/3.0L petrol, 5.0L SVR variant Jaguar XJ (X351, 2009–2019) – 3.0L/5.0L V8 petrol, naturally aspirated and supercharged Jaguar XK/XKR (X150, 2006–2014) – 4.2L/5.0L V8, naturally aspirated and supercharged Known For (Brand‑wide): Supercharged AJ126 and AJ133 engines deliver exceptional performance but require proactive timing chain maintenance to avoid catastrophic jump Ingenium petrol units share the same timing chain vulnerability as their diesel siblings, compounded by carbon buildup on direct-injection intake valves High-pressure fuel pump failure on 2.0L Ingenium petrol is a frequent pre-failure symptom prompting replacement searches Classic 4.0L/4.2L V8 owners value original engine numbers, driving demand for rebuilds over exchange units Typical Petrol Models (UK): XE P250 (AJ200P) XF 3.0 V6 Supercharged (AJ126) F-Type P450/P575 (AJ133/AJ133S) F-Pace SVR 5.0 V8 (AJ133S) XJ 5.0 V8 Supercharged (AJ133S) XKR 4.2/5.0 Supercharged (AJV8/AJ133)
items.cta: Compare Jaguar Petrol engine prices

items.title: Jaguar Plug‑in Hybrid (PHEV) Engines
items.description: Short descriptor: Jaguar's PHEV powertrains pair the 2.0L Ingenium petrol engine with an electric motor and high-voltage battery, appearing in the F-Pace P400e and E-Pace PHEV from 2018 onward, with specialist service required for any engine replacement. Common Jaguar PHEV Engine Families: AJ200P-PHEV – 2.0L Ingenium petrol + electric motor (F-Pace P400e, E-Pace PHEV), shares combustion engine failure modes with standard Ingenium How Jaguar PHEV Systems Work (brand‑wide): 48‑volt starter‑generator and high‑voltage battery provide electric‑only driving up to ~30 miles WLTP Regenerative braking recharges the battery; petrol engine engages seamlessly for longer journeys or high-load demands Engine replacement requires hybrid‑certified specialists to safely isolate high‑voltage components and recalibrate the energy management system Found In: Jaguar F-Pace P400e (X761, 2020–present) – 2.0L Ingenium petrol + electric motor, combined 404hp Jaguar E-Pace PHEV (X540, 2021–present) – 2.0L Ingenium petrol + electric motor, combined ~309hp Important Notes (Brand‑wide): The combustion engine block is identical to non‑hybrid Ingenium units, so timing chain and HPFP upgrades apply equally Battery health inspection is mandatory during any PHEV engine replacement to avoid mismatched system performance Specialist suppliers with JLR diagnostic access are required to reset hybrid system adaptations post‑fit
items.cta: Enquire about Jaguar PHEV engine availability

items.title: Jaguar Electric Powertrains
items.description: Short descriptor: Jaguar's fully electric I-Pace features dual permanent-magnet synchronous motors driving front and rear axles, with motor failure being rare but bearing wear and inverter issues prompting specialist exchange-unit replacements. Common Jaguar Electric Motors: EV300/EV400 – Front and rear drive units (I-Pace, 2018–present), combined output 394–400hp depending on variant How Jaguar Electric Powertrains Work: Dual-motor all-wheel drive delivers instant torque with no traditional gearbox; regenerative braking recovers energy to the 90kWh battery pack WLTP range ~292 miles; real-world typical range 220–260 miles depending on driving style and climate Motor failure is extremely rare; bearing whine, drive-unit noise, or inverter faults are more common replacement triggers Found In: Jaguar I-Pace (X590, 2018–present) – EV300 (early) and EV400 (facelift) drive units Important Notes: No traditional "engine" to replace – we connect you with HV-certified specialists for motor or inverter exchange units Labour rates for EV powertrain work differ from combustion engines; always confirm workshop certification before proceeding Exchange motors typically come from JLR-approved centres to maintain software compatibility and warranty coverage

engineCodeDirectory.h2: Jaguar Engine Codes – Most Replaced Engines (2008–2025)
engineCodeDirectory.intro: Every major Jaguar engine code from the last two decades, grouped by family, with compatible models and typical reasons why owners seek replacement. Select your engine to compare rebuilt prices and get quotes from UK Jaguar specialists.

entries.title: AJ200D 2.0L Diesel
entries.description: Powers XE (X760), XF (X260), F-Pace (X761) and E-Pace (X540) from 2015 onwards. The most frequently replaced Jaguar engine, notorious for timing chain stretch and severe oil dilution caused by aggressive DPF regeneration cycles. Quality rebuilt units feature upgraded timing chain kits, reinforced tensioners, and revised oil pump assemblies to eliminate the factory weak points.
entries.cta: Get quotes for AJ200D Jaguar engine replacement

entries.title: 204DTD 2.0L Diesel
entries.description: Found in early XE, XF and E-Pace models (2015–2018) producing 150–180hp. Shares the same timing chain architecture as the AJ200D but with lower power output, making it highly susceptible to cold-start chain rattle before catastrophic snap. Reconditioned examples include reinforced piston rings, updated ECU mapping to reduce DPF regeneration frequency, and machined block decks.

entries.title: 204DTA 2.0L Diesel
entries.description: High-output variant (180–240hp) used in XF Sportbrake, F-Pace and later XE models. Increased cylinder pressures accelerate injector and turbo wear, leading to compression loss and excessive oil consumption. Rebuilt units undergo complete valve seat machining, fitted with new piezo injectors, and include reinforced turbochargers for extended longevity.

entries.title: AJ20D6 3.0L Diesel
entries.description: Six-cylinder Ingenium diesel powering XJ (X351), F-Pace and XF (2017–present). Known for connecting rod bearing knock and crankshaft scoring due to oil starvation during extended service intervals. Specialist rebuilds replace main and big-end bearings with uprated shells, install revised oil gallery baffles, and include full dyno validation.

entries.title: AJ200P 2.0L Petrol
entries.description: Fits XE, XF, E-Pace and F-Pace petrol variants from 2017 onwards. High-pressure fuel pump (HPFP) failures and premature timing chain guide wear are the primary drivers for replacement, especially on pre-2020 units. Reconditioned engines feature upgraded HPFP assemblies, reinforced nylon chain guides, and revised camshaft phasers.
entries.cta: Get quotes for AJ200P Jaguar replacement engine

entries.title: 204PT 2.0L Petrol
entries.description: Lower-output 200hp Ingenium petrol used in early XE and XF saloons. Suffers from severe carbon buildup on intake valves and exhaust manifold cracking, leading to misfires and noticeable power loss. Rebuilt units include cleaned and ported cylinder heads, stainless steel exhaust manifolds, and updated positive crankcase ventilation systems.

entries.title: 224DT 2.2L Diesel
entries.description: The 2.2 TD4 Duratorq unit fitted to XF (X250) between 2011–2015. Catastrophic failure is almost always caused by copper injector washer degradation, allowing diesel to wash into the sump and destroy main bearings. Every rebuilt 224DT includes new copper injector seals, ultrasonically cleaned oil galleries, and fully reground crankshafts.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 224DT Jaguar engine replacement

entries.title: 306DT 3.0L V6 Diesel
entries.description: First-generation AJD-V6 3.0 diesel used in XF and XJ (2008–2012). Prone to piezo injector short-circuiting and EGR cooler leaks that cause white smoke and eventual hydrolock. Reconditioned variants supply remanufactured injector bodies, new EGR cooler cores, and reinforced cylinder head bolts.

entries.title: 306DTA 3.0L V6 Diesel
entries.description: Higher-output (240–275hp) iteration powering later XF, XJ and early F-Pace models. Turbo actuator sticking and intercooler pipe splits are common, but the real threat is camshaft bearing wear from delayed oil pressure at cold start. Specialist rebuilds fit uprated cam followers, revised turbo wastegate actuators, and balanced rotating assemblies.

entries.title: 274DT 2.7L V6 Diesel
entries.description: Predecessor to the 3.0 TDV6, found in early XF and S-Type (X200, 2008–2011). Less prone to crankshaft failure than Land Rover equivalents, but still suffers from EGR valve carbonisation and turbo compressor wheel erosion. Rebuilt units include machined EGR passages, balanced turbo assemblies, and updated coolant bypass valves.

entries.title: AJ126 3.0L V6 Petrol
entries.description: Supercharged V6 powering F-Type (X152), XF, XJ and F-Pace S models. Hydraulic timing chain tensioners lose pressure over time, causing chain slap and eventual jump past TDC on cold starts. Quality rebuilt units feature reinforced hydraulic tensioners, upgraded chain guides, revised oil pump pressure relief valves, and balanced crank pulleys.
entries.cta: Get quotes for AJ126 Jaguar engine replacement

entries.title: AJ133 5.0L V8 Petrol
entries.description: Naturally aspirated and supercharged V8 found in XF, XJ, XK and F-Pace SVR. Supercharger isolator couplers disintegrate, while timing chain tensioner failure causes catastrophic valve-to-piston contact. Reconditioned AJ133 engines include new composite couplers, reinforced timing rails, upgraded water pumps, and full dyno compression testing.

entries.title: AJ133S 5.0L V8 Petrol (High Output)
entries.description: 550–575hp variant reserved for F-Type SVR, XJR575 and Project 8. Extreme thermal cycling causes head gasket degradation and piston ring land cracking under track-style driving. Specialist rebuilds use MLS head gaskets, forged connecting rods, upgraded oil coolers, and balanced crankshafts for track-ready reliability.

entries.title: 5.0 V8 NA (AJ133 Base)
entries.description: Naturally aspirated version powering XJ, XF and XK base models. Lower thermal stress extends life, but timing chain guides still degrade past 80,000 miles, causing rattling and oil pressure drops. Rebuilt units include reinforced plastic chain guides, upgraded tensioners, and revised piston skirt coatings to reduce friction.
entries.cta: Get quotes for 5.0L NA Jaguar replacement engine

entries.title: AJV8 4.2L Petrol
entries.description: Naturally aspirated and supercharged engines in XK, XKR, XJ and XJR (2002–2009). Timing chain tensioner collapse and early Nikasil liner degradation are the main failure points. Reconditioned units feature steel sleeve liners, uprated tensioner pistons, and refreshed coolant crossover pipes.

entries.title: AJ26/AJ27 4.0L Petrol
entries.description: Early AJ-V8 units fitted to XJ8 and XK8 (1997–2002). Sulphur in modern fuel attacks Nikasil cylinder coatings, causing scoring and excessive oil consumption. Rebuilt engines are sleeved with high-strength iron liners, fitted with updated piston rings, and include revised oil baffle trays.

entries.title: AJ200D Hybrid (PHEV)
entries.description: Plug-in hybrid system pairing the 2.0L Ingenium diesel with an electric motor in F-Pace and E-Pace. Combustion engine suffers from the same timing chain issues as standard variants, complicated by high-voltage system integration. Replacement units come pre-calibrated for hybrid ECU communication and include upgraded thermal management modules.

entries.title: EV300 Electric Drive Unit
entries.description: Front and rear permanent magnet motors powering the I-Pace EV400. Bearing whine from coolant ingress and stator winding delamination are the primary failure modes. Replacement involves JLR-approved exchange units with updated lip seals, refreshed reduction gearing, and full software integration calibration. Closing line (Block A): Can’t find your engine code listed above? engineYears.h2: Jaguar Engines by Year – What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers Jaguar's complete UK engine timeline from 1997 to the present across every model line - from the AJ-V8 petrol heritage era through Ford-sourced diesels, the landmark 5.0-litre supercharged V8, and the Ingenium family - so owners can identify exactly what was fitted to their vehicle by year and compare rebuilt Jaguar engine prices from UK specialists.

years.preview: AJ-V8 Launch & Ford Partnership Begins 🔴 Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: Short intro: 1997 marked Jaguar's definitive break from the AJ16 inline-six era with the debut of the all-aluminium AJ-V8, while the Ford era simultaneously introduced V6 and diesel options across the new X-Type and S-Type ranges. Key Changes AJ-V8 3.2/4.0 all-aluminium V8 introduced (1997) - replaces the AJ16 inline-six across XK8, XJ, and S-Type Supercharged AJ-V8 4.0 launched in XKR and XJR (1998) - Jaguar's first supercharged V8 production engine 🔴 Performance Landmark AJ-V6 (Ford Duratec-derived) 2.5/3.0 introduced in S-Type (1999) and X-Type (2001) Ford AJD-V6 2.0 diesel first offered in X-Type (2001) - Jaguar's first diesel engine in volume production Euro 2/3 transition across the range Main Engines AJ-V8 (naturally aspirated) - 3.2/4.0 petrol V8 (XK8 X100, XJ X308, S-Type) AJ-V8SC - 4.0 supercharged petrol V8 (XKR, XJR, S-Type R) AJ-V6 (AJ25/AJ30) - 2.5/3.0 petrol V6 (S-Type, X-Type) AJ20 - 2.0 petrol V6 (X-Type 2.0) AJD-V6 (Ford/PSA) - 2.0/2.2 diesel V6 (X-Type diesel) Popular Models Jaguar XK8 / XKR (X100) Jaguar XJ (X308) Jaguar S-Type Jaguar X-Type Known For AJ-V8 naturally aspirated unit widely regarded as one of the smoothest and most characterful V8s of its generation - heritage-grade refinement, regularly achieves 150,000+ miles with disciplined servicing Nikasil cylinder bore lining on very early AJ-V8 units (pre-1999) susceptible to sulphur corrosion from older UK pump fuel - a known issue now largely confined to pre-1999 examples Supercharged 4.0 AJ-V8 in XKR/XJR a genuine performance landmark but valley pipe coolant hose perishing and thermostat failure are the two most commonly cited maintenance concerns Ford-sourced AJD-V6 diesel in X-Type competent but never considered a Jaguar-engineered unit - injector and DPF reliability varies at high mileage [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: AJ-V8, AJ28, AJ30, AJ25, AJ20, AJ-V6, AJD-V6, AJ4
years.cta: Find rebuilt 1997–2002 Jaguar AJ-V8 and AJ-V6 engine prices from UK specialists

years.preview: XJ X350 All-Aluminium Platform & S-Type R Peak 🔴 Performance Landmark
years.description: Short intro: The all-aluminium XJ (X350) brought the AJ-V8 into a groundbreaking new body structure, while the S-Type R and XKR delivered the supercharged 4.2 at its most refined - the pinnacle of the Ford-era supercharged V8. Key Changes AJ-V8 enlarged to 4.2-litre NA and 4.2SC supercharged (2003) - replaces 4.0 units across XK, XJ, and S-Type XJ X350 all-aluminium monocoque launched (2003) - first aluminium-intensive luxury saloon in volume production S-Type R 4.2SC introduced - Eaton M112 supercharger, 400 hp AJ-V6 3.0 continues in X-Type and S-Type alongside 2.5 AJD-V6 Ford PSA diesel 2.0/2.2 continues in X-Type Euro 3 in force; Euro 4 preparation begins Main Engines AJ-V8 4.2 NA - 4.2 naturally aspirated petrol V8 (XJ X350, XK8, S-Type 4.2) AJ-V8 4.2SC - 4.2 supercharged petrol V8 (XJR X350, XKR X150 preview, S-Type R) AJ-V6 3.0/2.5 - petrol V6 (S-Type 2.5/3.0, X-Type 2.5/3.0) AJD-V6 - 2.0/2.2 diesel (X-Type) Popular Models Jaguar XJ (X350) Jaguar S-Type R Jaguar XK8 / XKR Jaguar X-Type Known For AJ-V8 4.2SC in S-Type R and XJR universally respected as one of the most bulletproof supercharged engines Jaguar produced - PistonHeads and Jaguar forums consistently rate it the most reliable of all the V8 supercharged variants Thermostat housing failure and coolant valley pipe perishing on 4.2 units are the most common UK specialist enquiries - relatively low-cost preventative repairs X350 all-aluminium XJ considered a landmark engineering achievement but aluminium body corrosion at steel penetration points can create electrical grounding faults over time - not engine-related but affects ownership experience AJD-V6 diesel X-Type remains a poor relation in the Jaguar range - Ford/PSA architecture, not considered specialist-grade by the Jaguar community [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: AJ-V8 4.2NA, AJ-V8 4.2SC, AJ33, AJ34, AJ-V6 3.0, AJD-V6 2.2
years.cta: Compare 2003–2005 Jaguar 4.2 supercharged engine prices from UK rebuilders

years.preview: XK X150 & XF X250 Launch, 2.7 TDV6 Diesel Era Begins 🔴 Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: Short intro: The XF (X250) arrived as Jaguar's most important new model in a decade, but it also debuted the Ford-PSA 2.7 TDV6 diesel - a unit that would become the most searched-for Jaguar engine replacement in the UK. Key Changes XK X150 launched (2006) with AJ-V8 4.2 NA and 4.2SC - new aluminium body, existing engine family carried over XF X250 launched (2007) - replaces S-Type; offered with 2.7 TDV6, 3.0 V6 petrol, and 4.2 V8 2.7 TDV6 (Ford-PSA Lion V6 platform) introduced to Jaguar range via XF - 207 hp, twin-turbo diesel AJD-V6 2.2 diesel continues in X-Type (final years) Euro 4 enforced; Euro 5 preparation begins for 2009 Main Engines 2.7 TDV6 (276DT) - 2.7 twin-turbo diesel V6 (XF 2.7d) AJ-V8 4.2 NA - 4.2 naturally aspirated V8 (XK, XF 4.2, XJ) AJ-V8 4.2SC - 4.2 supercharged V8 (XKR, XFR preview, XJR) AJ-V6 3.0 - 3.0 petrol V6 (XF 3.0, S-Type final) AJD-V6 2.2 - 2.2 diesel (X-Type final years) Popular Models Jaguar XF (X250) Jaguar XK / XKR (X150) Jaguar XJ (X350) Jaguar X-Type (final) Known For 2.7 TDV6 widely described by UK specialists and owners as the most problematic diesel Jaguar ever sold - crankshaft design has inherent weak points between rod bearing journals and counterweights, leading to spun bearings and catastrophic failure; oil pump tensioner mount fracture is a separate common failure mode PistonHeads community consensus is that the 2.7 TDV6 was "horrendous" in long-term ownership - many XFs required full engine replacement within 80,000–100,000 miles AJ-V8 4.2SC XKR and XF 4.2 continue to be the most reliable and desirable engines in the range during this period XF launch generates enormous replacement engine search volume in the UK - 2.7 TDV6 replacement is among the highest-demand Jaguar engine queries to date [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: 276DT, AJ-V8 4.2NA, AJ-V8 4.2SC, AJ-V6 3.0, AJD-V6 2.2, AJ34, AJ35
years.cta: Get quotes for 2006–2008 Jaguar engines - 2.7 TDV6 and 4.2 V8 specialists listed

years.preview: 5.0-Litre V8 Family, 3.0 TDV6, and XFR/XJR Performance Peak 🔴 Major Engine Family Introduction 🔴 Performance Landmark
years.description: Short intro: 2009 was Jaguar's most significant engine year since 1997 - the new 5.0-litre AJ-V8 arrived in naturally aspirated and supercharged form, the 3.0 TDV6 replaced the troubled 2.7, and the XFR became Jaguar's definitive performance saloon. Key Changes 5.0 AJ-V8 naturally aspirated and 5.0SC supercharged introduced (2009) - entirely new cylinder block, bore, and stroke versus 4.2 🔴 Major Engine Family Introduction XFR and XKR 5.0SC launch with 510 hp - performance landmark for the Jaguar range 🔴 Performance Landmark 3.0 TDV6 (306DT) replaces 2.7 TDV6 - increased capacity, twin-turbo, revised oil pump, improved tensioner; addresses many 2.7 architecture concerns Euro 5 compliance from 2011 - DPF standard on all Jaguar diesels XJ X351 all-aluminium saloon launched (2009) with 3.0 TDV6 and 5.0 V8 Main Engines 5.0 AJ-V8 NA - 5.0 naturally aspirated V8 (XK 5.0, XJ 5.0, XF 5.0) 5.0 AJ-V8 SC - 5.0 supercharged V8 510 hp (XFR, XKR, XJR) 3.0 TDV6 (306DT) - 3.0 twin-turbo diesel V6 (XF 3.0d, XJ 3.0d, XK - ) AJ-V6 3.0 petrol - continued in XF and XJ until 2012 Popular Models Jaguar XF (X250) Jaguar XFR Jaguar XK / XKR (X150) Jaguar XJ (X351) Jaguar XJR Known For 5.0SC supercharged V8 is the definitive Jaguar performance engine - specialists and enthusiasts consistently rate it among the finest V8s ever built; however early (2009–2011) units have known timing chain and tensioner wear from 80,000 miles - many have been updated with revised later components 3.0 TDV6 (306DT) substantially more reliable than the 2.7 it replaced - oil pump redesigned and tensioner reinforced, but crankshaft inherits structural narrowness of the 2.7 platform and crankshaft/bearing failure remains a genuine risk at high mileage with poor service history 5.0 NA naturally aspirated unit considered very robust but water pump failure and timing chain noise are the most common UK specialist reports from 2014 onwards as cars age DPF oil dilution on 3.0 TDV6 during DPF regeneration cycles is a well-documented trait - not a fault but requires strict oil service intervals; diesel runaway risk on cars with excessive oil dilution [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: AJ-V8 5.0NA, AJ-V8 5.0SC, AJ133, AJ126, 306DT, 306DTA, AJ-V6 3.0
years.cta: Source 2009–2012 Jaguar 5.0 V8 and 3.0 TDV6 rebuilt engine quotes today

years.preview: F-Type Launch, XF/XJ Facelift & Pre-Ingenium Final Year
years.description: Short intro: The F-Type arrived as Jaguar's most exciting new model in a generation, stretching the 5.0SC to 550 hp in V8 S form, while the XF and XJ continued on established engines ahead of the full Ingenium transition. Key Changes F-Type launched (2013) - 3.0 supercharged V6 (340/380 hp) and 5.0SC V8 (495/550 hp) 3.0 AJ-V6SC supercharged petrol V6 introduced for F-Type - first use of supercharged V6 in Jaguar range XF and XJ receive Euro 6 preparation updates on 3.0 TDV6 diesel F-Type R launched with 550 hp 5.0SC - highest output Jaguar road engine at that point 🔴 Performance Landmark Final year of 2.2 diesel (AJD-V6 derivative) in XF before Ingenium replaces it Main Engines 5.0SC AJ-V8 - 5.0 supercharged V8 495/550 hp (F-Type V8/V8 S, XFR, XKR-S) 3.0 AJ-V6SC - 3.0 supercharged V6 340/380 hp (F-Type V6/V6 S) 3.0 TDV6 (306DT) - 3.0 diesel V6 (XF, XJ) 2.2 diesel (AJ4D) - 2.2 diesel four-cylinder (XF 2.2d - final iteration) Popular Models Jaguar F-Type (X152) Jaguar XF (X250) Jaguar XJ (X351) Jaguar XK / XKR (X150) Known For F-Type launch is a genuine Jaguar heritage moment - 5.0SC V8 S delivers benchmark supercar performance in a precision-engineered GT package; 550 hp F-Type R widely considered one of the most exciting Jaguars ever built 3.0 AJ-V6SC in F-Type more accessible entry point to the range - supercharger nose section and bearing wear on higher-mileage examples (from 60,000 miles) is the primary specialist concern 2.2 diesel XF generates increasing replacement enquiry as fleet and ex-lease examples pass 80,000 miles - timing chain and DPF among the most searched issues 5.0SC timing chain concerns on 2009–2012 cars become more prominent in UK specialist enquiries as those vehicles age into this period [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: AJ-V8 5.0SC, AJ133, AJ126, 306DT, 306DTA, AJ-V6SC 3.0, AJ4D 2.2
years.cta: Get 2013–2015 Jaguar engine replacement quotes - F-Type, XF, and XJ specialists

years.preview: Ingenium Engine Family Launch Across XE, XF & F-Pace 🔴 Major Engine Family Introduction
years.description: Short intro: 2015–2016 marked Jaguar's most significant engineering investment since the AJ-V8 debut - the in-house Ingenium four-cylinder family replaced all Ford-sourced four-cylinder engines, launching first in the new XE and then rolling across XF and F-Pace. Key Changes Ingenium AJ20D4 2.0 diesel introduced (2015) - fully in-house Jaguar Land Rover architecture; replaces Ford-sourced 2.2 in XE, XF, and F-Pace 🔴 Major Engine Family Introduction Ingenium AJ20P4 2.0 petrol turbo introduced (2016) - replaces Ford-sourced 2.0 petrol in XE and XF XE (X760) launched (2015) as Jaguar's first compact executive saloon - Ingenium-only engine offering F-Pace (X761) launched (2016) - Jaguar's first SUV; 2.0 and 3.0 diesel options 3.0 TDV6 and 5.0SC V8 continue in XF (G-generation), XJ, and F-Type Euro 6 enforced from September 2015 on all new registrations Main Engines Ingenium AJ20D4 - 2.0 diesel four-cylinder (XE, XF G-gen, F-Pace) Ingenium AJ20P4 - 2.0 turbocharged petrol four-cylinder (XE, XF) 3.0 TDV6 (306DT/306DTA) - 3.0 diesel V6 (XF, XJ, F-Pace 3.0d) 5.0SC AJ-V8 - 5.0 supercharged V8 (F-Type R, XFR, XJR) 3.0 AJ-V6SC - 3.0 supercharged V6 (F-Type V6 S) Popular Models Jaguar XE Jaguar XF (X260) Jaguar F-Pace Jaguar F-Type Jaguar XJ (X351) Known For Ingenium AJ20D4 marks Jaguar's break from Ford-sourced diesel architecture - fuel economy and refinement highly praised; however early EGR cooler cracking and timing chain wear on early 2015–2016 units are the most reported specialist concerns Ingenium timing chain failure on 2.0 diesel is the single highest-growth Jaguar replacement engine search in the UK from 2020 onwards as the first-generation XE and XF fleet ages - chain replacement alone can cost £1,500–£3,000 at UK specialists AJ20P4 petrol competent but OPF (Otto Particle Filter) from Euro 6 and oil dilution on short-journey driving flagged by independent specialists 3.0 TDV6 crankshaft failure enquiries peak during this period as pre-2012 fleet ages past the 100,000-mile threshold - reinforcing the 306DT as the most-replaced Jaguar diesel engine in the UK [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: AJ20D4, AJ200D, AJ20P4, AJ200P, 306DT, 306DTA, AJ-V8 5.0SC, AJ133, AJ-V6SC 3.0

years.preview: Ingenium Matures, E-Pace & I-Pace, Euro 6d Compliance 🔴 Technology Introduction

Key Changes I-Pace (X590) launched (2018) - Jaguar's first fully electric vehicle; twin electric motors, 90kWh battery 🔴 Technology Introduction E-Pace (X540) launched (2018) - compact SUV; Ingenium 2.0 diesel and petrol only Euro 6d-TEMP enforcement - AdBlue/SCR added to all Ingenium diesel applications from 2018–2019 Ingenium AJ20D4 revised (2018) with updated EGR, injector, and timing chain tensioner specification F-Type facelift (2020) - 5.0SC continues; 2.0 Ingenium petrol added as entry powertrain Main Engines Ingenium AJ20D4 (revised) - 2.0 diesel with SCR/AdBlue (XE, XF, F-Pace, E-Pace) Ingenium AJ20P4 - 2.0 turbocharged petrol (XE, XF, E-Pace, F-Pace, F-Type entry) 3.0 TDV6 (306DTA) - 3.0 diesel V6 (XF, F-Pace 3.0d - final applications) 5.0SC AJ-V8 - 5.0 supercharged V8 (F-Type R/SVR, XFR) Jaguar Electric Drive - twin motor 400 hp (I-Pace) Popular Models Jaguar F-Pace Jaguar E-Pace Jaguar XF (X260) Jaguar I-Pace Jaguar F-Type Known For I-Pace launch positions Jaguar as a credible EV contender - acclaimed for driving dynamics and design, though battery degradation and 12V battery failure have become growing UK independent specialist topics as early cars age Revised Ingenium AJ20D4 addresses early EGR and tensioner concerns - 2018-onwards units have notably improved UK specialist feedback versus 2015–2016 examples AdBlue pump and injector failure emerging as a new failure category on post-2018 Ingenium diesels as these vehicles approach the 60,000–80,000-mile mark 3.0 TDV6 enters its final years in Jaguar applications - replacement demand for this unit remains strong from the large existing UK fleet of XF and F-Pace 3.0d owners [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: AJ20D4, AJ200D, AJ20P4, AJ200P, 306DTA, AJ-V8 5.0SC, Jaguar Electric Drive 294kW

Key Changes Ingenium AJ30D6 3.0 inline-six diesel introduced - replaces Ford-PSA 3.0 TDV6 (306DT) across XF and F-Pace 🔴 Major Engine Family Introduction Ingenium AJ30P6 3.0 inline-six petrol introduced - powers F-Pace SVR, XF P400 PHEV variants introduced: XF P400e and F-Pace P400e (2021) - Ingenium 2.0 petrol + electric motor 🔴 Technology Introduction Euro 6d final compliance - revised OBD and RDE real-world emissions testing on all engines F-Type R final series runs out 5.0SC V8 as Jaguar signals move to all-electric future Main Engines Ingenium AJ30D6 - 3.0 inline-six diesel (XF, F-Pace 3.0d) Ingenium AJ30P6 - 3.0 inline-six petrol (F-Pace SVR, XF P400) Ingenium AJ20P4 PHEV - 2.0 petrol + 105kW electric motor (XF P400e, F-Pace P400e) Ingenium AJ20D4 - 2.0 diesel (E-Pace, XE final, F-Pace entry) 5.0SC AJ-V8 - 5.0 supercharged V8 (F-Type R, final editions) Popular Models Jaguar F-Pace (X761) Jaguar XF (X260) Jaguar F-Type R (final) Jaguar E-Pace Jaguar I-Pace Known For AJ30 inline-six Ingenium diesel is a precision-engineered step forward from the Ford-PSA 3.0 V6 - specialists and early long-term users report strong reliability, though it remains too recent for definitive high-mileage assessment PHEV battery health on XF P400e and F-Pace P400e becoming an emerging UK concern - high-voltage battery degradation and electric motor integration maintenance are specialist-grade tasks outside most franchised dealer budgets F-Type 5.0SC V8 final editions generate significant collector and specialist interest - replacement engine demand for ageing 2009–2015 examples is accelerating as running costs on older units increase Jaguar's announcement of full transition to electric vehicles (Reimagine strategy, 2021) creates urgency in UK combustion engine replacement market - owners retaining existing models driving increased demand for rebuilt and remanufactured units [Tags] Engine Codes Covered: AJ30D6, AJ300D6, AJ30P6, AJ300P6, AJ20P4 PHEV, AJ20D4, AJ-V8 5.0SC, AJ133

items.question: What are the most common Jaguar engine failures?
items.answer: The most common Jaguar engine problems include timing chain stretch on AJ200D/204DTD Ingenium diesels, severe oil dilution from aggressive DPF regeneration, and tensioner wear on the 3.0L supercharged petrol family. You will typically notice cold-start rattling, rising dipstick oil levels, or sudden pressure drops before internal damage becomes irreversible. When left unchecked, these issues rarely respond to minor repairs, making full replacement the only viable long-term solution for owners tracking Jaguar engine failure symptoms.
